Viewed $200M Boost for Ukraine Air Defense Brings Canada’s 🇨🇦 PURL Support to $830M

Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Canada has contributed more than $830 million to support Ukraine’s air defense efforts under the PURL (Prioritized Ukraine Requirements List) initiative. The announcement followed a meeting with Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney.

In a statement published on Telegram on May 4, after their talks on the sidelines of the European Political Community Summit in Yerevan, Zelenskyy thanked Canada for its continued backing and highlighted a newly announced contribution.

“Additional $200 million, announced today by Prime Minister Mark Carney, is an important contribution to protecting our people from constant Russian ballistic strikes. In total, Canada has already allocated more than $830 million to support this initiative. These are decisions that truly save lives,” Zelenskyy said.

The Ukrainian president added that the discussions also focused on strengthening bilateral cooperation, securing further support for Ukraine, and intensifying efforts to return Ukrainian children who were forcibly taken by Russia.

Additionally, Canadian Foreign Minister Anita Anand has recently announced a new package of sanctions targeting 100 vessels linked to Russia’s so-called “shadow fleet.”

Viewed Ukrainian 🇺🇦 Drones Destroy Two 50,000 m³ Oil Tanks at Russian Pumping Station

Ukrainian long-range drones struck the “Gorky” oil pumping station in Russia’s Nizhny Novgorod region, destroying two large storage tanks and damaging critical filtration infrastructure.

According to the OSINT monitoring group Dnipro Osint on May 4, satellite images confirm that two oil reservoirs, each with a capacity of 50,000 cubic meters, were destroyed during the strike.

The same source reported additional damage to the station’s filtration systems and possible destruction of warehouse facilities. The site is operated by Transneft–Upper Volga, a subsidiary of Russia’s state pipeline monopoly.

The strike targeted infrastructure essential to the preparation and transportation of crude oil. The “Gorky” station functions both as a primary and intermediate node within Russia’s pipeline network, facilitating oil flows from Western Siberia through routes such as Surgut–Gorky–Polotsk toward refineries in central Russia and for export.

The facility is located near the town of Meshikha, close to Kstovo. Following the April 23 attack, a fire was reported at the site, with subsequent satellite imagery indicating significant structural damage to oil storage and processing components.

The strike comes amid a broader campaign targeting Russia’s oil infrastructure, which has led to prolonged disruptions at multiple facilities.

Viewed ঔদ্ধত্য মানুষ ক্ষমা করেন না।

দলীয় রাজনীতি নিয়ে আমরা কথা বলিনা, কিন্তু এই চ্যানেলের অনেক পর্বেই আমি চেষ্টা করি রাজনৈতিক সচেতনতা প্রসারের। কারণ রাজনীতি প্রত্যেক মানুষের জীবনের সঙ্গে ওতপ্রোতভাবে জড়িত। আমাদের নিজেদের স্বার্থে আমাদের রাজনীতি-সচেতন হওয়া অপরিহার্য।

এই নির্বাচন আমার কাছে এই জন্য অভূতপূর্ব কারণ এবার মানুষ স্বতঃস্ফূর্তভাবে গণতন্ত্রে অংশ নিয়েছেন। তাঁদের নিজেদের স্বার্থরক্ষার তাগিদে ভোট দিয়েছেন। এই সচেতনতা প্রসারিত হোক।

ইতিহাস বারবার প্রমাণ করেছে ঔদ্ধত্য মানুষ ক্ষমা করেন না। মানুষই শাসককে নির্বাচন করেন, তাই মানুষই নির্ধারণ করবেন নেতা-মন্ত্রীরা কতদিন মানুষের সেবার দায়িত্বে থাকবেন।

এই ইতিহাস বারবার ঘুরেফিরে আসবে। বাংলার মানুষকে অভিনন্দন, চলুন আমরা সচেতন, দায়িত্বশীল নাগরিকের ভূমিকা পালন করি। যাঁদের হাতে আজ দায়িত্ব তুলে দিচ্ছি, তাঁদের ভয় নয়, কর্তব্য স্মরণ করাই, ভুল মনে হলে সমালোচনা করি, ভালো কাজের প্রশংসা করি। তবে, একমাত্র তবেই গণতন্ত্র পরিণত হবে, আমরা ভালো থাকবো। নইলে আবার যে-কে সেই!

Viewed Ukraine 🇺🇦 Builds Frontline Medevac on Battle-Tested Inguar-3 With Rapid-Load Ramp

Ukraine’s defense industry has introduced a new medical evacuation variant of the Inguar-3 armored vehicle, designed to transport wounded personnel from the battlefield under protection.

According to Defense Express on May 4, Ukrainian manufacturer Inguar Defence presented the Inguar-3 MED as a dedicated medevac platform based on its combat-proven armored vehicle already in service with Ukraine’s Defense Forces.

The new version replaces the standard rear troop doors with a fast-loading ramp equipped with guide rails, allowing quicker and more controlled loading of stretchers.

Inside, the vehicle has been reconfigured from a troop compartment into a medical module, featuring mounting points for stretchers and space for specialized equipment. The layout is modular, enabling adaptation depending on mission requirements.

The Inguar-3 MED is designed to carry two wounded personnel at a time, prioritizing stability and medical support during transport rather than maximum capacity.

The vehicle retains the base platform’s protection, rated at Level 3 under STANAG 4569, along with its independent suspension and high-mobility chassis suited for rough terrain.

According to Defense Express, the Inguar-3 platform has already demonstrated survivability in combat conditions and serves as a base for multiple variants, including troop transport, drone operator vehicles, and other specialized configurations.

Viewed Ukraine’s 🇺🇦 FP-1/2 Drone Spotted Carrying FPV Drones With Possible Starlink Integration

A previously unseen configuration of Ukraine’s FP ½ long-range drone—equipped with two smaller FPV drones mounted on its wings—has been identified for the first time, suggesting a new operational concept involving airborne deployment of strike drones.

Footage published by Russian sources shows a downed Ukrainian FP ½ drone discovered in water, likely in a bay near temporarily occupied Crimea. The video, recorded by a Russian FPV drone, reveals that the aircraft was carrying two additional FPV drones attached to its structure.

According to Defense Express on May 2, one of the smaller drones appears to be fitted with a flat antenna resembling a compact Starlink terminal. The second drone does not visibly feature such equipment, possibly due to damage sustained during the crash.

The presence of a Starlink antenna indicates that the FPV drone may have operated with direct satellite communication rather than relying on the FP ½ as a signal relay.

This configuration suggests the FP ½ could function primarily as a carrier platform—delivering FPV drones into the target area before continuing its own mission. This would allow for multi-layered strikes, where smaller drones engage mobile or high-value targets, such as air defense units or interception teams, clearing the way for follow-on attacks.

According to Defense Express, even with two FPV drones onboard, the FP ½ may still retain a significant payload capacity. Based on comparisons with other variants capable of reaching Crimea with warheads exceeding 100 kilograms, the payload in this configuration could be reduced but still substantial.

Viewed Кардиохирург из Польши за день пересадил два сердца и вошел в историю

В сентябре 1987-го весь мир облетела фотография National Geographic, на которой уставший хирург следит за жизненными показателями пациента, в то время как его изможденный ассистент спит прямо на полу операционной. За этим снимком стояла непростая история польского доктора Збигнева Религи — человека, который смог не просто помочь сотням людей, но и изменить отношение общества к пересадке сердца.

Самый влиятельный кардиохирург Польши в молодости хирургом быть не планировал. Тем не менее Релига окончил мединститут и отправился на стажировку в США. Возможно, именно там он по–настоящему загорелся профессией. Религе предлагали работу за границей, но он вернулся строить карьеру в Польшу. Сделав себе имя, Збигнев решил доказать стране, что пересадка сердца — это не святотатство и не пустая трата времени. В то время в социалистической Польше было крайне сложно достать современное высокотехнологичное оборудование. К тому же, сама операция, в ходе которой еще бьющееся сердце пересаживали другому человеку, в католическом обществе воспринималась как глубоко неэтичная.

В 1984 году хирург переехал из Варшавы в Забже и создал с нуля Силезский центр сердечных заболеваний. Вместе со своей командой Релига сам отделал здание и навел порядок внутри. В ноябре 1985–го он провел там первую в Польше операцию по пересадке сердца. За ней пошли другие. Релига доказал эффективность своего метода, и его клинике, наконец, выделили госфинансирование. 

В сентябре 1987–го Збигнев с ассистентами за сутки провел в Силезском центре две трансплантации. Та самая фотография National Geographic была сделана глубокой ночью после окончания второй операции — доктор пересадил сердце 60-летнему учителю Тадеушу Житкевичу. Фото было признано одним из ста лучших в истории издания. 

Позже Релига первым в Польше подключил больному искусственное сердце, а также попробовал себя в политике. В 2005 его назначили министром здравоохранения страны. Но Збигневу пришлось уйти в отставку из–за рака легких. Релига скончался в 2009 году. Многие из его пациентов пережили своего хирурга, в том числе тот самый Тадеуш Житкевич, который прожил еще 30 лет после операции и умер в возрасте 91 года в 2017 г.

Viewed I saw a post on Reddit recently where someone cut their cloud usage, but their bill didn't change at all. It’s a common trap called the Efficiency Paradox. Let me explain.

Think of it like buying a massive 3-year supply of milk to get a bulk discount, but then you realize you’re going dairy-free next month. You’re still stuck paying for all that milk you won’t drink. In the cloud, this happens when you sign a long-term Savings Plan to get a discount, but then you delete your old servers to be more efficient. You end up paying for "zombie capacity" that does nothing.

👉 Check out Archera: https://fandf.co/4tBt6Y4

They offer insured commitments for this exact problem. You get the deep 3-year discount, but if your needs change or you migrate to new tech, they cover the cost of what you don’t use. It’s a solid way to keep your bill low without being locked into a contract, which is huge if you're trying to stay agile with things like GenAI. Shoutout to them for sponsoring this post.

Pretty cool I think.

Viewed At 15, world number one junior. 
At 20, a World Championship bronze. 

At 22, the first Olympic semifinal any Indian male badminton player had ever reached. 

Numbers that no male Indian shuttler had ever touched. 

In our recent episode of Figuring Out, I talked to Lakshya Sen, the 23-year-old who carried India's biggest badminton dream all the way to Paris. 

He was one match away from a medal. One match away from everything he had worked his entire life for.

But he lost that match. 

And post that, he locked himself in his room. Even when he came back to compete, he kept losing tournament after tournament. 

This was despite the fact that he was training every single day, but mentally, he was just not there.

Then, finally, he won the “Australian Open”. 

How do you lose the thing you spent 20 years chasing and still find the courage to start again?

In this conversation, we figure out what goes on inside the mind of a champion when everything is falling apart.

Full episode out now, watch it here: https://www.figuringout.co/link/fo-504

A striking billboard in Tehran has gone viral for its bold political messaging, depicting Donald Trump with his mouth sealed using imagery of the Strait of Hormuz, a powerful symbol of control over one of the world’s most critical oil routes. The installation reflects escalating tensions between Iran and the US, where the waterway has become central to both economic pressure and geopolitical signaling.

The visual is part of a broader wave of state-backed messaging in Tehran, using public spaces to project political narratives during the ongoing standoff. Viewed Germany 🇩🇪 to Boost Defense Spending to 3.1% of GDP, Allocates €11.6 Billion for Ukraine

The German government approved key targets for its 2027 draft budget, allocating €11.6 billion ($13.57 billion) in direct support for Ukraine, Reuters reported on April 29.

This commitment is part of a medium-term financial framework that pledges €8.5 billion ($9.95 billion) annually between 2028 and 2030.

Total defense spending in the 2027 budget is set to reach €144.9 billion ($169.53 billion) when special defense fund and earmarked aid for Ukraine are included. Chancellor Friedrich Merz noted that recent global developments have underscored the necessity of investing in defense capabilities, bringing Germany’s total military expenditure to 3.1% of its gross domestic product in 2027.

The 2027 draft budget allocates total spending of €543.3 billion ($635.66 billion), an increase of 3.6% over the previous year. To finance these objectives and revitalize the economy, the government plans total borrowing of €196.5 billion ($229.91 billion), departing from years of fiscal conservatism to address infrastructure and security needs, Reuters reported.

Germany’s increased defense spending for 2027 coincides with a significant pivot toward unmanned technology. The German military had recently signed a framework agreement with Rheinmetall, valued at up to €1 billion ($1.17 billion), to acquire FV-014 loitering munitions. The first €300 million ($351 million) order includes roughly 2,500 units designed to strike targets up to 100 kilometers away.

Viewed Ukraine 🇺🇦 Hits Russian Karakurt-Class Missile Ship in Primorsk Port

Ukraine’s Defense Forces struck a Russian missile ship capable of launching Kalibr cruise missiles during a drone attack on the port of Primorsk overnight on May 3, targeting multiple military and energy-related assets.

According to a statement by Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy published on May 3, the operation resulted in damage to a Russian Karakurt-class missile ship, as well as a patrol boat and a tanker linked to Russia’s so-called shadow oil fleet.

Zelenskyy said he had been briefed by acting head of the Security Service of Ukraine (SBU), Major General Yevhenii Khmara, on the outcome of the strike.

“Each such result limits Russia’s war potential. I approved the SBU’s additional, entirely justified responses to Russian strikes on our cities and villages. Russia can end this war at any moment. Prolonging the war will only lead to the expansion of our defensive operations,” Zelenskyy stated.

The strike was carried out as a joint operation involving the SBU, the Unmanned Systems Forces, the Special Operations Forces, Ukraine’s military intelligence (HUR), and border guard units.

Viewed What’s one thing you know you SHOULD be doing right now but you keep delaying?

Most people already know what would improve their life.

Working out.
Starting that idea.
Fixing their routine.
Making that call.
Learning that skill.

The problem is not lack of information.
The problem is discomfort.

Because growth is uncomfortable.

Being a beginner is uncomfortable.
Consistency is uncomfortable.
Doing the hard thing when nobody is forcing you to do it is uncomfortable.

So slowly, people choose comfort instead.

And that’s where average life begins.

The people who grow in life are not always the smartest or most talented.

They just stop running away the moment things feel difficult.

Because the truth is:
Everything meaningful feels slow before it feels rewarding.

Your future is probably hidden inside the thing you keep postponing.

So here’s a small challenge for today:

Think about the ONE thing you’ve been avoiding lately.
That one call.
That workout.
That idea.
That difficult conversation.
That first step.

And train your mind to start it TODAY.

Comment “READY” if you’re done waiting for the perfect moment 👇

Viewed Have you watched our new report on the GUR "Bratstvo" Battalion yet?

In this video, we reveal the details of one of the most daring special operations conducted by Ukrainian military intelligence. The fighters crossed the dried bed of the Kakhovka reservoir, moving 30 kilometers deep behind enemy lines completely undetected.

Their mission? To capture Russian officers alive, extract critical intelligence, and – using captured enemy radios – deceive Russian special forces to make it back to safety. It sounds like a Hollywood thriller, but this is the absolute reality of war.

The film also features intense FPV drone operations stopping infiltration groups on the Zaporizhzhia front. Tragically, since this story was filmed, one of the brave fighters featured in the raid, Anton “Umnik” Poliukhovych, was killed in action. This film honors his memory.

Viewed Ukraine 🇺🇦 to Deploy 8,000 Octopus Interceptor Drones Against Russian Attacks

The Ukrainian Defense Forces are set to receive 8,000 Octopus-100 interceptor drones designed to strengthen air defense and increase the success rate of neutralizing aerial targets.

Developed within the Armed Forces of Ukraine, the Octopus drone utilizes an automatic guidance system specifically to intercept Shahed-type loitering munitions, according to Ukraine’s Minister of Defense, Mykhailo Fedorov.

The production programme already includes 29 Ukrainian companies that have received licences, along with the British government. Four domestic enterprises have already signed state contracts to supply these drones to the military.

The project moved forward following a government decision to transfer military technologies to Ukrainian manufacturers while maintaining ownership rights. Licenses were granted to the defense holding TAF Industries and the electronic warfare manufacturer UNWAVE.

According to the executive director of TAF Industries, a primary challenge to scaling production was the reliance on several critical components. These parts were in limited supply, which prevented a rapid increase in manufacturing speed. To address this, developers and manufacturers worked together to find replacements for the scarce components.

Viewed Pentagon 🇺🇸 Releases $400 Million in Ukraine, Defense Secretary Conforms

Pentagon has released $400 million in funding for Ukraine that had already been authorized by Congress, following criticism from senior Republican lawmakers over delays in disbursing the money, as reported by Bloomberg on April 29.

Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth confirmed the move during a House Armed Services Committee hearing on Wednesday, stating that the financing had been “allocated for European capacity building, and as of yesterday it was released,” without providing further details.

The delay had drawn public criticism from Senator Mitch McConnell of Kentucky, who chairs the Senate’s defense appropriations panel. In an opinion piece published in The Washington Post, McConnell wrote, “The Ukraine aid we passed months ago is now collecting dust at the Pentagon.”

“When Senate appropriators have sought an explanation from the department’s policy shop, led by Undersecretary Elbridge Colby, they’ve been stonewalled,” he added.

According to Bloomberg, the release comes amid broader shifts in US policy toward Ukraine. Support has slowed as President Donald Trump and Hegseth have called on European allies to take on a larger share of the financial burden for Ukraine’s defense against Russia.

Viewed Ukraine’s 🇺🇦 Long-Range Strikes Cost Russia $7 Billion in Oil Revenues Since January

Drone strikes on Russian oil refineries and infrastructure have cost Russia at least $7 billion since the beginning of the year, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy stated on May 1.

Summarizing the results of operations conducted throughout April, Zelenskyy said that Ukraine’s “long-range sanctions” reached a new level across three key components: reducing Russian oil profits, increasing the distance of the strikes, and raising the overall intensity of the attacks.

The president emphasized that these strikes are designed to maximize facility downtime and cause severe operational delays. According to Zelenskyy’s conservative estimates, hits, prolonged downtime, and delayed shipments have drained at least $7 billion directly from the Russian oil and refining industry since January.

Zelenskyy credited the coordinated efforts of the Ukrainian Armed Forces, the Security Service of Ukraine (SBU), and national intelligence agencies for delivering these results.

Looking ahead, the president confirmed that Kyiv intends to expand these operations. “We will scale up the direction of our long-range systems,” Zelenskyy said, noting that specific decisions to facilitate this expansion are currently being prepared.

Viewed Japan 🇯🇵 May Finance Ukrainian Air Defense Development To Replace US Patriots

Japan may provide financial backing for Ukraine’s development of a domestic air defense system, a move designed to reduce Kyiv’s reliance on increasingly scarce US-made Patriot missiles.

In an interview with Reuters on May 1, Ukraine’s Ambassador to Tokyo, Yuriy Lutovinov, emphasized the potential for a strategic partnership between the two nations.

“We are not the country that would like to just ask. We are the country that is going to provide as well. The technology ​of Japan and experience of Ukraine, if we can put them together, it would be a high-class product,” Lutovinov stated.

Beyond direct development, discussions are reportedly underway regarding Japan’s potential involvement in PURL program. This mechanism, which has already facilitated over $4 billion in weaponry and ammunition for Kyiv, opened to non-NATO members last year with the participation of Australia and New Zealand.

“Every country can participate in this mechanism, following its own legal framework. It can also be non-lethal weapons,” the ambassador noted.

Furthermore, the diplomat suggested that Japanese firms could play a vital role in diversifying the supply chains for electronics and micro-components essential for the thousands of drones deployed on the front lines.

Viewed Ukrainian Forces 🇺🇦 Strike Russian Su-57 Jets and Su-34 Aircraft At Airbase 1700 Km Deep Inside Russia

Ukraine’s Armed Forces have struck advanced Russian military aircraft at a base deep inside Russian territory, according to a statement from the General Staff of the Armed Forces of Ukraine on May 1.

The operation, carried out on April 25, targeted the Shagol airfield in Russia’s Chelyabinsk region. Ukrainian Unmanned Systems Forces reportedly hit several Sukhoi Su-57 fighter jets as well as a Sukhoi Su-34 aircraft.

The General Staff said the strike was part of ongoing efforts to reduce Russia’s capacity to launch attacks on civilian infrastructure across Ukraine. The extent of the damage to the aircraft is still being assessed, the statement said.

According to the Ukrainian military, the targeted airfield is located approximately 1,700 kilometers from Ukraine’s state border.

Sri Lanka legend Muttiah Muralitharan sparked debate by saying that making pitches more balanced in the IPL could actually reduce fan interest. He argued that T20 audiences prefer high-scoring games filled with fours and sixes, making entertainment the priority over balance.

According to him, the format is designed for spectacle, not necessarily fairness between bat and ball, highlighting the evolving nature of modern T20 cricket.

and I genuinely felt a sense of grounding I didn’t expect.

Not loud, not showy,
just quiet strength, discipline and a deep sense of duty.

What stayed with me the most was the humility. 
The way history isn’t worn as pride, but as responsibility. 
The way spirituality isn’t spoken about, but lived.

Walking through City Palace Udaipur, you don’t just see heritage, you feel it. 

In the silence, in the stories, in the energy of that land.

Thank you for your time, sir. 
For reminding me that true royalty isn’t about status, it’s about character, sacrifice and staying anchored to something far greater than yourself 🇮🇳

Viewed “The wise see with equal vision…” – Bhagavad Gita 5.18 

True wisdom is not just knowledge—it is the ability to see beyond external differences. A realized soul views all beings with equal respect: a learned scholar, a cow, an elephant, a dog, or even one considered low in society. This vision arises from understanding the divine presence within every living being.

In a world divided by labels, status, and appearances, this verse reminds us to rise above superficial distinctions and cultivate humility, compassion, and unity. When we begin to see the same soul in all, our actions naturally become kinder, our hearts more peaceful, and our lives more meaningful.

Let this wisdom guide us today—to treat everyone with dignity and recognize the divine in all. 🙏

#SwamiMukundananda #BhagavadGita #GitaWisdom #SpiritualGrowth #Equality #Oneness #Vedanta #InnerPeace #SelfRealization #Wisdom #DailyInspiration #SwamiMukundanadaVideos

Viewed In 2020, when we first started reviewing smartphones, we always thought — you buy a phone, give your candid opinion, people watch it and make a wise buying decision.

In 2026, due to some brands, things have come down to: "buy a phone" → "write a review" → "get it verified from a lawyer" → change your wordings so it doesn't defame the brand when it comes to court.

Growing up as a channel means we affect so many people's buying decisions and thought process. So it comes with a lot of pressure — which I genuinely love and respect. But 2026 has taught me about a different kind of pressure: the legal pressure of making a video.

Today, I feel we are standing at a very pivotal stage of YouTube — where a video can make or break a brand. And honestly, it sucks to have to legally verify your script before publishing. If this continues, it just kills YouTube reviews as we know them.
In my entire career, I've loved launches, tech, smartphones. Not sleeping for days to ship videos on time — and enjoying every bit of it. But this particular phase of YouTube? It just sucks.

On a fun note — buy our channel membership --> https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCdp6GUwjKscp5ST4M4WgIpw/join 🫶

Three years ago, a fire started that shook the entire country. Today, that fire has not just returned… it never really went away. This time, it has taken the lives of innocent children. And once again, the state is slipping back into chaos.

But the real question is, what happened in these three years? What changed? And more importantly… what did not?

In today’s video, I break down the entire Manipur crisis. From how it started in 2023, to what has unfolded since then, and why even now, there seems to be no real solution.

Viewed JUST FEW HOURS TO NEET and you all are feeling lost 

Not because of the exam…
but because of everything attached to it —
expectations, past mistakes, time you think you “wasted”, and the fear of “what if I couldn’t do it”.

And somewhere in between all this, you’ve made a quiet decision about yourself—
“Maybe I’m not good enough.”

But think about this carefully—

If you were truly “not capable”…
you wouldn’t feel this much pressure.
You wouldn’t care this much.
You wouldn’t still be here, thinking about tomorrow.

This pressure you feel?
It’s not proof of failure.
It’s proof that this matters to you.

And when something matters, your mind tries to protect you—
by assuming the worst, so it doesn’t get hurt later.

But that protection becomes limitation.

Tomorrow is not asking you to be perfect.
It’s asking you to be honest.

Honest attempt. Honest focus. Honest effort for those 3 hours.

Not “fix my whole year in one paper.”
Just—do what you can, as clearly as you can.

And here’s the part no one says—

Even if you feel underprepared…
there will still be questions you can solve.
There will still be marks you can take.

Don’t let one thought—
“I didn’t do enough”—
steal marks from the things you do know.

Because that’s how papers are actually lost.

So tomorrow, don’t go to prove anything.
Don’t go to fight your past.

Just go, sit down, and respond to what’s in front of you.

That’s enough.

And whatever happens after that—
we’ll deal with it.

Together. All the best ❤️
